text: Lesson 24
zh: 第24课

text: A skeleton in the cupboard
zh: 壁橱里的骷髅

text: Who was Sebastian?
zh: 塞巴斯蒂安是谁?

text: We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person
zh: 我们常常在小说中读到,一个看似体面的人

text: or family has some terrible secret which has been concealed from strangers for years.
zh: 或一个家庭,竟隐藏着某个多年不为外人所知的可怕秘密。

text: The English language possesses a vivid saying to describe this sort of situation.
zh: 英语里有一个生动的说法用来形容这种情形。

text: The terrible secret is called 'a skeleton in the cupboard'.
zh: 这种可怕的秘密被称为“壁橱里的骷髅”。

text: At some dramatic moment in the story, the terrible secret becomes known and a reputation is ruined.
zh: 在故事的某个戏剧性时刻,这个可怕的秘密被揭穿,一个人的名誉便毁于一旦。

text: The reader's hair stands on end when he reads in the final pages of the novel that the heroine,
zh: 当读者读到小说的最后几页,发现女主人公——

text: a dear old lady who had always been so kind to everybody, had, in her youth, poisoned every one of her five husbands.
zh: 那位一向待人和蔼可亲的可爱老太太,年轻时竟毒死了自己的五任丈夫中的每一个——不禁毛骨悚然。

text: It is all very well for such things to occur in fiction.
zh: 这种事发生在小说里当然无妨。

text: To varying degrees, we all have secrets which we do not want even our closest friends to learn,
zh: 我们每个人多多少少都有一些秘密,连最亲密的朋友也不愿透露,

text: but few of us have skeletons in the cupboard.
zh: 但我们当中,壁橱里藏着骷髅的人却寥寥无几。

text: The only person I know who has a skeleton in the cupboard is George Carlton, and he is very proud of the fact.
zh: 我认识的唯一一个壁橱里真有骷髅的人是乔治·卡尔顿,而他对此还颇为得意。

text: George studied medicine in his youth.
zh: 乔治年轻时学过医。

text: Instead of becoming a doctor, however, he became a successful writer of detective stories.
zh: 但他后来没有当医生,而是成了一位成功的侦探小说作家。

text: I once spent an uncomfortable weekend which I shall never forget at his house.
zh: 有一次我在他家度过了一个令人难受、终生难忘的周末。

text: George showed me to the guestroom which, he said, was rarely used.
zh: 乔治把我领到客房,说这间房很少有人用。

text: He told me to unpack my things and then come down to dinner.
zh: 他让我先整理行李,再下楼吃饭。

text: After I had stacked my shirts and underclothes in two empty drawers, I decided to hang one of the two suits I had brought with me in the cupboard.
zh: 我把衬衫和内衣放进两个空抽屉后,决定把随身带来的两套西装中的一套挂进壁橱。

text: I opened the cupboard door and then stood in front of it petrified.
zh: 我打开壁橱门,顿时吓得呆立当场。

text: A skeleton was dangling before my eyes.
zh: 一具骷髅正悬在我眼前晃动。

text: The sudden movement of the door made it sway slightly and it gave me the impression that it was about to leap out at me.
zh: 开门的猛然动作让它微微摇晃起来,给我一种它要扑向我的错觉。

text: Dropping my suit, I dashed downstairs to tell George.
zh: 我扔下西装,冲下楼去告诉乔治。

text: This was worse than 'a terrible secret'; this was a real skeleton!
zh: 这可比“可怕的秘密”更糟糕——这是一具真正的骷髅!

text: But George was unsympathetic.
zh: 但乔治毫不同情。

text: 'Oh, that,' he said with a smile as if he were talking about an old friend.
zh: “哦,那个啊,”他微笑着说,仿佛在谈论一位老朋友。

text: 'That's Sebastian. You forget that I was a medical student once upon a time.'
zh: “那是塞巴斯蒂安。你忘了,我曾经也当过医学院学生呢。”
